POSIX says that implementations may, but not must, make sa_sigaction overlap with sa_handler, but we know of no implementation where they do not overlap. */ void (*_sa_sigaction) (int, siginfo_t *, void *); } _sa_func; sigset_t sa_mask; /* Not all POSIX flags are supported. */ int sa_flags; };

types.name: _gl_verify_type
types.declaration: struct _gl_verify_type { unsigned int _gl_verify_error_if_negative: W; }. If W is negative, this yields a compile-time error. No compiler can deal with a bit-field of negative size. One might think that an array size check would have the same effect, that is, that the type struct { unsigned int dummy[W]; } would work as well. However, inside a function, some compilers (such as C++ compilers and GNU C) allow local parameters and variables inside array size expressions. With these compilers, an...

macros.name: END_ESC

macros.definition: #define _C_CTYPE_PUNCT \ case '!': case '"': case '#': case '$': \ case '%': case '&': case '\'': case '(': \ case ')': case '*': case '+': case ',': \ case '-': case '.': case '/': case ':': \ case ';': case '<': case '=': case '>': \ c...

macros.name: INT_REMAINDER_RANGE_OVERFLOW
macros.definition: #define INT_REMAINDER_RANGE_OVERFLOW(a, b, min, max) \ INT_DIVIDE_RANGE_OVERFLOW (a, b, min, max)

macros.name: _GL_BINARY_OP_OVERFLOW
macros.definition: #define _GL_BINARY_OP_OVERFLOW(a, b, op_result_overflow) \ op_result_overflow (a, b, \ _GL_INT_MINIMUM (_GL_INT_CONVERT (a, b)), \ _GL_INT_MAXIMUM (_GL_INT_CONVERT (a, b)))

macros.name: INT_ADD_OK
macros.definition: #define INT_ADD_OK(a, b, r) (! INT_ADD_WRAPV (a, b, r))

macros.name: INT_SUBTRACT_OK
macros.definition: #define INT_SUBTRACT_OK(a, b, r) (! INT_SUBTRACT_WRAPV (a, b, r))

macros.name: INT_MULTIPLY_OK
macros.definition: #define INT_MULTIPLY_OK(a, b, r) (! INT_MULTIPLY_WRAPV (a, b, r))

macros.name: _GL_INT_OP_CALC
macros.definition: #define _GL_INT_OP_CALC(a, b, r, op, overflow, ut, t, tmin, tmax) \ (overflow (a, b, tmin, tmax) \ ? (*(r) = _GL_INT_OP_WRAPV_VIA_UNSIGNED (a, b, op, ut, t), 1) \ : (*(r) = _GL_INT_OP_WRAPV_VIA_UNSIGNED (a, b, op, ut, t), 0))

macros.name: UC_SURROGATE
macros.definition: #define UC_SURROGATE UC_CATEGORY_Cs /* all of them are invalid characters */

macros.name: UC_UNASSIGNED
macros.definition: #define UC_UNASSIGNED UC_CATEGORY_Cn /* some of them are invalid characters */

macros.name: CHMOD_MODE_BITS
macros.definition: #define CHMOD_MODE_BITS \ (S_ISUID | S_ISGID | S_ISVTX | S_IRWXU | S_IRWXG | S_IRWXO)
